texts.label: text
texts.orig: Per the Veteran-focused Integrated Process (VIP) Guide, the User’s Guide is required to be completed prior to Critical Decision Point #2 (CD2), with the expectation that it will be updated as needed. A User Guide is a technical communication document intended to give assistance to people using a particular system, such as VistA end users. It is usually written by a technical writer, although it can also be written by programmers, product or project managers, or other technical staff. Most user guides contain both a written guide and the associated images. In the case of computer applications, it is usual to include screenshots of the human-machine interfaces, and hardware manuals often include clear, simplified diagrams. The language used is matched to the intended audience, with jargon kept to a minimum or explained thoroughly. The User Guide is a mandatory, build-level document, and should be updated to reflect the contents of the most recently deployed build. The sections documented herein are required if applicable to your product.
texts.text: Per the Veteran-focused Integrated Process (VIP) Guide, the User’s Guide is required to be completed prior to Critical Decision Point #2 (CD2), with the expectation that it will be updated as needed. A User Guide is a technical communication document intended to give assistance to people using a particular system, such as VistA end users. It is usually written by a technical writer, although it can also be written by programmers, product or project managers, or other technical staff. Most user guides contain both a written guide and the associated images. In the case of computer applications, it is usual to include screenshots of the human-machine interfaces, and hardware manuals often include clear, simplified diagrams. The language used is matched to the intended audience, with jargon kept to a minimum or explained thoroughly. The User Guide is a mandatory, build-level document, and should be updated to reflect the contents of the most recently deployed build. The sections documented herein are required if applicable to your product.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1 Introduction
texts.text: 1 Introduction

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.1 Purpose
texts.text: 1.1 Purpose

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this guide is to provide instructions on how to use the Provider Role Tool (PRT) application.
texts.text: The purpose of this guide is to provide instructions on how to use the Provider Role Tool (PRT) application.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool enables authorized users to reassign future alerts for
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool enables authorized users to reassign future alerts for

texts.label: text
texts.orig: qualifying patient orders
texts.text: qualifying patient orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: from a current provider to one or more new providers. It handles the cases where a provider changes roles while remaining at the same site (for example, a provider who moves from VA to DOD but does not relocate or a resident who rotates from one specialty service to another, i.e.: Oncology to Surgery). The goal is for the current provider to no longer receive notifications for orders written in the previous role, while being able to receive notifications for orders written in the new role. The purpose is to improve patient care by having alerts available to the provider now responsible for continuing that patient’s treatment or therapy.
texts.text: from a current provider to one or more new providers. It handles the cases where a provider changes roles while remaining at the same site (for example, a provider who moves from VA to DOD but does not relocate or a resident who rotates from one specialty service to another, i.e.: Oncology to Surgery). The goal is for the current provider to no longer receive notifications for orders written in the previous role, while being able to receive notifications for orders written in the new role. The purpose is to improve patient care by having alerts available to the provider now responsible for continuing that patient’s treatment or therapy.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: One way to think of the Provider Role Tool is to consider it a more advanced version of the CPRS Surrogates feature. The Surrogates feature lets you redirect all notifications from one provider to another – useful when one provider is on vacation. Provider Role Tool redirects only selected notifications – those related to qualifying orders issued while the current provider was acting in a now-discontinued role or capacity at a given site.
texts.text: One way to think of the Provider Role Tool is to consider it a more advanced version of the CPRS Surrogates feature. The Surrogates feature lets you redirect all notifications from one provider to another – useful when one provider is on vacation. Provider Role Tool redirects only selected notifications – those related to qualifying orders issued while the current provider was acting in a now-discontinued role or capacity at a given site.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool application swaps “qualifying” patient orders from an original provider to a substitute provider. The reassignment ensures that the original provider won’t receive any notifications associated with the orders in question, but that the substitute provider will receive them.  (A “qualifying” patient order is an open order issued by the original provider during a specified time period --by default, the last calendar year).
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool application swaps “qualifying” patient orders from an original provider to a substitute provider. The reassignment ensures that the original provider won’t receive any notifications associated with the orders in question, but that the substitute provider will receive them.  (A “qualifying” patient order is an open order issued by the original provider during a specified time period --by default, the last calendar year).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool implements VA New Service Request (NSR) 20130504. The program is independent from CPRS but works with CPRS GUI servers.
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool implements VA New Service Request (NSR) 20130504. The program is independent from CPRS but works with CPRS GUI servers.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NSR 20130504 was written because of a provider who moved from VA to DOD while remaining at the same site. After the role change, the provider was no longer looking after VA patients, and was instead responsible for DOD patients. This role change put the facility into a difficult situation. If they assigned a surrogate for the original provider, that surrogate received order notifications for the original provider’s VA
texts.text: NSR 20130504 was written because of a provider who moved from VA to DOD while remaining at the same site. After the role change, the provider was no longer looking after VA patients, and was instead responsible for DOD patients. This role change put the facility into a difficult situation. If they assigned a surrogate for the original provider, that surrogate received order notifications for the original provider’s VA

texts.label: text
texts.orig: and
texts.text: and

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DOD patients. If they did not assign a surrogate, the original provider continued to receive notifications for VA patients. They needed a solution that would let the original provider move on to new duties without receiving notifications for orders issued in the abandoned VA role.
texts.text: DOD patients. If they did not assign a surrogate, the original provider continued to receive notifications for VA patients. They needed a solution that would let the original provider move on to new duties without receiving notifications for orders issued in the abandoned VA role.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.1.1 Assumptions
texts.text: 1.1.1 Assumptions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users:
texts.text: Users:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool will be used by personnel that sites designate to reassign responsibility from one provider to another. It is assumed that using Provider Role Tool will be an administrative function, perhaps performed by a Clinical Application Coordinator (CAC). The CAC can perform this function under the direction of clinical personnel who can speak to how qualifying orders will be reassigned to one or more different providers. However, it is up to sites to decide on the process to reassign orders.
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool will be used by personnel that sites designate to reassign responsibility from one provider to another. It is assumed that using Provider Role Tool will be an administrative function, perhaps performed by a Clinical Application Coordinator (CAC). The CAC can perform this function under the direction of clinical personnel who can speak to how qualifying orders will be reassigned to one or more different providers. However, it is up to sites to decide on the process to reassign orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Accessibility:
texts.text: Accessibility:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool is fully compliant with Section 508 accessibility directives.
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool is fully compliant with Section 508 accessibility directives.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1.1.2 Coordination
texts.text: 1.1.2 Coordination

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To use the Provider Role Tool, sites will need to coordinate the installation and make sure the correct users are authorized to use the software.
texts.text: To use the Provider Role Tool, sites will need to coordinate the installation and make sure the correct users are authorized to use the software.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For installation, sites need to coordinate their local resources with Information Technology Operations and Services (ITOPS) support. The site Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) Coordinator may be involved in selecting which users will need to use Provider Role Tool. It is anticipated that CACs will use the Provider Role Tool to reassign orders to new providers.
texts.text: For installation, sites need to coordinate their local resources with Information Technology Operations and Services (ITOPS) support. The site Computerized Patient Record System (CPRS) Coordinator may be involved in selecting which users will need to use Provider Role Tool. It is anticipated that CACs will use the Provider Role Tool to reassign orders to new providers.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2 System Summary
texts.text: 2 System Summary

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool is a client-server GUI application that enables users to reassign existing orders to another provider.  This reassignment often happens because a provider is changing roles but is remaining at the same location.  For example, a reassignment would happen if a provider was working for the VA but took a position with a Department of Defense co-located site.  The provider would still be at a VA site but would no longer be responsible for VA patients.
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool is a client-server GUI application that enables users to reassign existing orders to another provider.  This reassignment often happens because a provider is changing roles but is remaining at the same location.  For example, a reassignment would happen if a provider was working for the VA but took a position with a Department of Defense co-located site.  The provider would still be at a VA site but would no longer be responsible for VA patients.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool GUI is a front-end application that interacts with the VistA database using CPRS communication protocols.  It affects which users receive notifications.
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool GUI is a front-end application that interacts with the VistA database using CPRS communication protocols.  It affects which users receive notifications.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To launch the Provider Role Tool, users will either select an item from the CPRS Tools menu or activate it from an icon.
texts.text: To launch the Provider Role Tool, users will either select an item from the CPRS Tools menu or activate it from an icon.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Once logged in, the user can select the provider whose orders need to be changed and the date range for those orders. After selecting the provider, patients with orders from the selected provider display on the left side of the main PRT window.
texts.text: Once logged in, the user can select the provider whose orders need to be changed and the date range for those orders. After selecting the provider, patients with orders from the selected provider display on the left side of the main PRT window.

texts.orig: 3.1 Logging On
texts.text: 3.1 Logging On

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users can launch Provider Role Tool directly from an icon or from the CPRS Tools menu. If the site chooses to use Provider Role Tool from the Tools menu, CACs or other support personnel will need to add the menu item to CPRS.
texts.text: Users can launch Provider Role Tool directly from an icon or from the CPRS Tools menu. If the site chooses to use Provider Role Tool from the Tools menu, CACs or other support personnel will need to add the menu item to CPRS.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2.1 Logging in to the Stand-alone PRT through Two-Factor Authentication
texts.text: 3.2.1 Logging in to the Stand-alone PRT through Two-Factor Authentication

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can use your Personal Identification Verification (PIV) card and Personal Identification Number (PIN) to access PRT.
texts.text: You can use your Personal Identification Verification (PIV) card and Personal Identification Number (PIN) to access PRT.

texts.orig: To login to the Provider Role Tool with your PIV card:
texts.text: To login to the Provider Role Tool with your PIV card:

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Figure 3: Provider Role Tool Server Connection Dialog

texts.text: On the System Use Notification window, click “OK”.

texts.text: Note:

texts.text: After you click “OK”, you will see the PRT main window and will not need to enter your Access and Verify Codes.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4 Using the Software
texts.text: 4 Using the Software

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.1 Preparing for a Provider Role Change
texts.text: 4.1 Preparing for a Provider Role Change

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It’s important to properly research and prepare before attempting a provider role change. Remember that the Provider Role Tool, though it displays patient names, is reassigning
texts.text: It’s important to properly research and prepare before attempting a provider role change. Remember that the Provider Role Tool, though it displays patient names, is reassigning

texts.label: text
texts.orig: orders
texts.text: orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: , not patients. The purpose of the application is to ensure that new notifications for recently issued orders go to a newly designated provider(s).
texts.text: , not patients. The purpose of the application is to ensure that new notifications for recently issued orders go to a newly designated provider(s).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To successfully implement a provider role change using the Provider Role Tool, you should answer these questions before attempting a transfer:
texts.text: To successfully implement a provider role change using the Provider Role Tool, you should answer these questions before attempting a transfer:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Who is changing roles?
texts.text: Who is changing roles?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The management team identifies the provider changing roles.
texts.text: The management team identifies the provider changing roles.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: What patients have qualifying orders?
texts.text: What patients have qualifying orders?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The management team identifies patients who will be affected by the role change. Provider Role Tool defines these patients as those who have qualifying orders issued by the provider changing roles during a specified time period.
texts.text: The management team identifies patients who will be affected by the role change. Provider Role Tool defines these patients as those who have qualifying orders issued by the provider changing roles during a specified time period.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Who will receive order notifications for these qualifying orders?
texts.text: Who will receive order notifications for these qualifying orders?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The management team identifies the new provider(s) who will now manage the patients and orders formerly assigned to the departing provider.
texts.text: The management team identifies the new provider(s) who will now manage the patients and orders formerly assigned to the departing provider.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: How far back are we going?
texts.text: How far back are we going?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The management team also determines a time period covered by the transfer, such as “all orders from one year ago until today at midnight”.
texts.text: The management team also determines a time period covered by the transfer, such as “all orders from one year ago until today at midnight”.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: How will we allocate transfers between multiple new providers?
texts.text: How will we allocate transfers between multiple new providers?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The management team specifies how patients/orders will be allocated between new providers. This could be simple, as in “evenly divide patients between these three new providers”. Or it could be complex, as in “find all the cardiac patients and assign them to provider X and find all the orthopedic patients and assign them to provider Y”.
texts.text: The management team specifies how patients/orders will be allocated between new providers. This could be simple, as in “evenly divide patients between these three new providers”. Or it could be complex, as in “find all the cardiac patients and assign them to provider X and find all the orthopedic patients and assign them to provider Y”.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Are the designated new providers aware of this pending transfer?
texts.text: Are the designated new providers aware of this pending transfer?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It’s incumbent on the management team to get “buy-in” from the new providers that will suddenly be receiving order notifications for patients formerly managed by the previous provider.
texts.text: It’s incumbent on the management team to get “buy-in” from the new providers that will suddenly be receiving order notifications for patients formerly managed by the previous provider.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Are the designated new providers able to receive the appropriate order notifications?
texts.text: Are the designated new providers able to receive the appropriate order notifications?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can’t transfer orders to a new provider who isn’t able to receive order notifications.
texts.text: You can’t transfer orders to a new provider who isn’t able to receive order notifications.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Are you sure simple surrogacy is not sufficient?
texts.text: Are you sure simple surrogacy is not sufficient?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Provider Role Tool is designed to serve the business case where a provider is changing roles
texts.text: Provider Role Tool is designed to serve the business case where a provider is changing roles

texts.label: text
texts.orig: at the same site
texts.text: at the same site

texts.label: text
texts.orig: . If the provider is retiring or leaving the site, other business processes are probably more appropriate.
texts.text: . If the provider is retiring or leaving the site, other business processes are probably more appropriate.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Provider Role Tool can optionally assist in discovering patients and orders for a departing provider. For example, a user can enter a departing provider and a date range, and “assign” them to a new provider. In the Qualifying Orders window, Provider Role Tool will present a detailed listing of qualified orders for each patient. The management team can use this listing when designating which patients/orders go to each newly designated provider.
texts.text: The Provider Role Tool can optionally assist in discovering patients and orders for a departing provider. For example, a user can enter a departing provider and a date range, and “assign” them to a new provider. In the Qualifying Orders window, Provider Role Tool will present a detailed listing of qualified orders for each patient. The management team can use this listing when designating which patients/orders go to each newly designated provider.

texts.orig: Additional features in the Provider Role Tool GUI:
texts.text: Additional features in the Provider Role Tool GUI:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Most tables and lists have popup or context menus with additional actions. When in doubt, right click to see if a menu pops up.
texts.text: Most tables and lists have popup or context menus with additional actions. When in doubt, right click to see if a menu pops up.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The main screen supports drag and drop. You can drag patients from left to right or between providers in the right panel.
texts.text: The main screen supports drag and drop. You can drag patients from left to right or between providers in the right panel.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Some tables support double click. You can double click a patient to see associated orders.
texts.text: Some tables support double click. You can double click a patient to see associated orders.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Most tables and lists support multiple selections using standard Windows techniques.
texts.text: Most tables and lists support multiple selections using standard Windows techniques.

texts.text: 4.2.2 Buttons & Menu Items

texts.text: All buttons on the main window have corresponding menu commands in the “Edit” main menu. All buttons and menu items have shortcuts (command keys) that are displayed when the “ALT” key is pressed.

texts.text: Choose Current Provider and Order Dates

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : Displays a popup dialog (see additional help) in which the user can select the current provider from a list. The user can also modify the default selection period, which defaults to the past calendar year. Upon selection, the patients list at left screen will be populated with patients. The order count for each patient displays to the right of their names.
texts.text: : Displays a popup dialog (see additional help) in which the user can select the current provider from a list. The user can also modify the default selection period, which defaults to the past calendar year. Upon selection, the patients list at left screen will be populated with patients. The order count for each patient displays to the right of their names.

texts.orig: Notes:
texts.text: Notes:

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
The default “Orders Start Date” is one year ago from today and the default “Orders Stop Date” is the current date.  
The “Orders Start Date” can be any date in the past, but the “Orders Stop Date” cannot be a future date.
The dialog box does a search by last name, not by first or middle name. You can only type the name of one provider.
texts.text:
The default “Orders Start Date” is one year ago from today and the default “Orders Stop Date” is the current date.

The dialog box does a search by last name, not by first or middle name. You can only type the name of one provider.

texts.orig: 4.3.3 Move Patient Orders to a New Provider
texts.text: 4.3.3 Move Patient Orders to a New Provider

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The third phase of reassigning patient orders is to move the patient orders to new providers and apply the changes.
texts.text: The third phase of reassigning patient orders is to move the patient orders to new providers and apply the changes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the Provider Role Tool: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window, do the following:
texts.text: In the Provider Role Tool: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window, do the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click on a new provider.
texts.text: Click on a new provider.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click on a patient name.
texts.text: Click on a patient name.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 17: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window after a patient and new provider have been selected
texts.text: Figure 17: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window after a patient and new provider have been selected

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click the “Add Selected” button on the main window or select
texts.text: Click the “Add Selected” button on the main window or select

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Edit | Add Selected
texts.text: Edit | Add Selected

texts.label: text
texts.orig: on the menu.
texts.text: on the menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note:
texts.text: Note:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can assign all patients to a new provider by clicking on the “Add All” button or selecting
texts.text: You can assign all patients to a new provider by clicking on the “Add All” button or selecting

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Edit | Add All
texts.text: Edit | Add All

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click the “Apply Changes” button on the main window or select
texts.text: Click the “Apply Changes” button on the main window or select

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Edit | Apply Changes
texts.text: Edit | Apply Changes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: on the menu.
texts.text: on the menu.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 18: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window before “Apply Changes” has been clicked (highlighted in red for clarity)
texts.text: Figure 18: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window before “Apply Changes” has been clicked (highlighted in red for clarity)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.3.4 Reassign Orders
texts.text: 4.3.4 Reassign Orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The fourth phase of reassigning patient orders is to complete the Reassign Orders dialog, which makes the changes permanent.
texts.text: The fourth phase of reassigning patient orders is to complete the Reassign Orders dialog, which makes the changes permanent.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After the user has clicked the “Apply Changes” button, the Review Orders (Review and Execute) window will display.   Because this is an irreversible change, Provider Role Tool requires this deliberate review step. The Review and Execute window displays the orders that need to be reassigned, and the user must click the Reassign Orders button to complete the reassignment process.
texts.text: After the user has clicked the “Apply Changes” button, the Review Orders (Review and Execute) window will display.   Because this is an irreversible change, Provider Role Tool requires this deliberate review step. The Review and Execute window displays the orders that need to be reassigned, and the user must click the Reassign Orders button to complete the reassignment process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To complete the order reassignment:
texts.text: To complete the order reassignment:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Accept the default “Effective Date” of today or change to a future date.  The “Effective Date” cannot be a past date.
texts.text: Accept the default “Effective Date” of today or change to a future date.  The “Effective Date” cannot be a past date.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 19: Reassign Orders button, highlighted in red for clarity
texts.text: Figure 19: Reassign Orders button, highlighted in red for clarity

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Review the list of reassigned orders.
texts.text: Review the list of reassigned orders.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Note: 	The review screen will likely have hundreds or possibly thousands of individual orders. The tool presents the list and requires another button click mainly as a “stop and pause” feature.
texts.text: Note: 	The review screen will likely have hundreds or possibly thousands of individual orders. The tool presents the list and requires another button click mainly as a “stop and pause” feature.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click the “Reassign Orders” button to complete reassignment.
texts.text: Click the “Reassign Orders” button to complete reassignment.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When the Transfer Completed window appears, click “OK”.
texts.text: When the Transfer Completed window appears, click “OK”.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 20 – Transfer Completed window
texts.text: Figure 20 – Transfer Completed window

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Review the results to see if any reassignments failed.
texts.text: Review the results to see if any reassignments failed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Optionally copy the reassignment results to the Windows clipboard by right-clicking on the Reassign Orders (Review and Execute) screen and clicking “Copy Results to Clipboard”.
texts.text: Optionally copy the reassignment results to the Windows clipboard by right-clicking on the Reassign Orders (Review and Execute) screen and clicking “Copy Results to Clipboard”.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 21 – Copy Results to Clipboard
texts.text: Figure 21 – Copy Results to Clipboard

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Return to the main window by clicking the “X” in the top right corner.
texts.text: Return to the main window by clicking the “X” in the top right corner.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Below is a screen capture showing the text report pasted into Notepad.
texts.text: Below is a screen capture showing the text report pasted into Notepad.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 22: Notepad text report
texts.text: Figure 22: Notepad text report

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.4 How to View a Patient’s Orders
texts.text: 4.4 How to View a Patient’s Orders

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Once patients are listed in either pane of the main window, the user can bring up the Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window and view the details about the orders.
texts.text: Once patients are listed in either pane of the main window, the user can bring up the Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window and view the details about the orders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A qualifying order has the following characteristics:
texts.text: A qualifying order has the following characteristics:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: It was issued by the selected provider.
texts.text: It was issued by the selected provider.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: It was issued during the selected date range.
texts.text: It was issued during the selected date range.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: It is a signed order.
texts.text: It is a signed order.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: It is in a state identified as potentially generating a future notification.
texts.text: It is in a state identified as potentially generating a future notification.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window has three sections:
texts.text: The Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window has three sections:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Qualifying orders for the selected patient are listed on the left side of the window. Some orders may be in an expired or discontinued state, but these orders are still considered qualifying orders.
texts.text: Qualifying orders for the selected patient are listed on the left side of the window. Some orders may be in an expired or discontinued state, but these orders are still considered qualifying orders.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The details about an order are shown on the right side of the window.
texts.text: The details about an order are shown on the right side of the window.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The order transfer history is listed on the bottom right side of the window.
texts.text: The order transfer history is listed on the bottom right side of the window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To view a patient’s orders:
texts.text: To view a patient’s orders:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select a current or new provider on the Provider Role Tool: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window (the main window).
texts.text: Select a current or new provider on the Provider Role Tool: Reassign Signed Patient Orders window (the main window).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window, perform one of the following tasks:
texts.text: In the Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window, perform one of the following tasks:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Double-click on a patient on either the left or right pane.
texts.text: Double-click on a patient on either the left or right pane.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Right-click on a patient on either the left or right pane and then, click on “Show Orders for selected patient(s)”.
texts.text: Right-click on a patient on either the left or right pane and then, click on “Show Orders for selected patient(s)”.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To view the details and transfer history of a patient’s order, left-click, right-click or press the shift key on a patient’s name.
texts.text: To view the details and transfer history of a patient’s order, left-click, right-click or press the shift key on a patient’s name.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Click the “Close” button to go back to the main window.
texts.text: Click the “Close” button to go back to the main window.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 23: Qualifying Orders for Patient(s)
texts.text: Figure 23: Qualifying Orders for Patient(s)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4.5 How to Create an Audit Report
texts.text: 4.5 How to Create an Audit Report

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The audit report displays
texts.text: The audit report displays

texts.label: text
texts.orig: all
texts.text: all

texts.label: text
texts.orig: order reassignments that occurred within a specific time period at the VistA site that you are affiliated with.
texts.text: order reassignments that occurred within a specific time period at the VistA site that you are affiliated with.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You will see order reassignments in
texts.text: You will see order reassignments in

texts.label: text
texts.orig: all
texts.text: all

texts.label: text
texts.orig: statuses for
texts.text: statuses for

texts.label: text
texts.orig: all
texts.text: all

texts.label: text
texts.orig: patients transferred from and to
texts.text: patients transferred from and to

texts.label: text
texts.orig: all
texts.text: all

texts.label: text
texts.orig: providers at your VistA site within a specified time period.
texts.text: providers at your VistA site within a specified time period.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The audit report default Stop Date is the current date and the default Start Date is one year behind today’s date.
texts.text: The audit report default Stop Date is the current date and the default Start Date is one year behind today’s date.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: To create an audit report:
texts.text: To create an audit report:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: On the PRT Menu, select
texts.text: On the PRT Menu, select

texts.label: text
texts.orig: View | Audit Report
texts.text: View | Audit Report

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: On the Audit Report window, do the following:
texts.text: On the Audit Report window, do the following:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Select an “Audit Start Date” and an “Audit Stop Date”.  To set a new date, select the “Audit Start Date” or “Audit Stop Date” calendar and type a date.
texts.text: Select an “Audit Start Date” and an “Audit Stop Date”.  To set a new date, select the “Audit Start Date” or “Audit Stop Date” calendar and type a date.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Click the “Build Report” button.
texts.text: Click the “Build Report” button.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: To view details about an order, click on an order and then, click the “Show Orders” button.  You will then
texts.text: To view details about an order, click on an order and then, click the “Show Orders” button.  You will then

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: To view details about an order, click on the order and then, click the “Show Orders” button.  You will be taken to the Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window.
texts.text: To view details about an order, click on the order and then, click the “Show Orders” button.  You will be taken to the Qualifying Orders for Patient(s) window.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: To go back to the main window, click the “Close” button.
texts.text: To go back to the main window, click the “Close” button.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Figure 24: Audit Report
texts.text: Figure 24: Audit Report

texts.text: 5 Troubleshooting

texts.text: If the user receives an error stating they are not authorized to use Provider Role Tool, verify the user is assigned the OR PRT ACCESS key.
